# Transcrição: 29. Trait 28 - Great Salespeople focus on results not effort.

- **Fonte:** 29. Trait 28 - Great Salespeople focus on results not effort.
- **Duração:** 01:22
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:22]

Great sales people don't stay busy. They're not interested in busy. They're interested in productivity. They're interested in results. Look there's a big difference between the productive and the busy. I've done them both. I've been highly productive and I've been very busy before. What you want to do is be very, very busy but be extremely productive. This is what the greats do. They don't measure activities and efforts. They measure productivity. They don't task. They don't do task and list. The results in paperwork and organizing and preparing and filing. Look they're like hey somebody else take care of that. I need to go out into the marketplace. Make and build relationships. Increase my visibility in the marketplace. Get people to know me and get them to talk about me. Do business with me. Call me and send me business. You see that's a completely different level then. I gotta do some filing. We got to go to office depot and staples and buy some goodies. I got to go get another computer. Look that's busy. You need to be busy? You go home at night. Your kids ask you, did you sell something today Papa? No but I bought a computer and I got my desk all pretty. Look don't be busy. Be productive. Okay be productive and quit lying to yourself when you're just busy because that's what average normal people do. The greats are productive and they never let themselves off the hook.
